---
title: "Statistical Analysis II Homework"
author: "Omer Taha Kerem"
date: "2026-04-05"
output:
  word_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(readxl)

veri <- read_excel("paired_veri.xlsx", sheet = 3)

x <- veri$`Veri 69`

# [TR] Shapiro-Wilk normallik testi uygulanmıştır.
# [EN] Shapiro-Wilk normality test was applied.

shapiro.test(x)

# [TR] p-değeri > 0.05 olduğu için veri normal dağılım göstermektedir.
# [TR] Bu nedenle parametrik test (paired t-test) kullanılacaktır.

# [EN] Since p-value > 0.05, the data is normally distributed.
# [EN] Therefore, a parametric test (paired t-test) will be used.

once <- x[1:20]
sonra <- x[21:40]

# [TR] Bağımlı örneklem t-testi uygulanmıştır.
# [EN] Paired sample t-test was applied.

t.test(once, sonra, paired = TRUE)

# [TR] p-değeri > 0.05 olduğu için anlamlı fark yoktur.
# [EN] There is no statistically significant difference.
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
